---
title: "[SOLVED] Gutsy/Nvidia Compiz Fusion HUGE RAM load"
date: 2007-10-20
forum: Desktop Effects &amp; Customization
---

### Post by Kimmik on 2007-10-20
I installed Gutsy, and I use a Vaio laptop with an "Nvidia Geforce Go 6200".
Gutsy uses the new nvidia driver on default "nvidia-glx-new".
I have a problem with compiz fusion, the ram usage is enormous (300+ MB)!

My xorg.conf

```
# nvidia-xconfig: X configuration file generated by nvidia-xconfig
# nvidia-xconfig:  version 1.0  (buildmeister@builder3)  Wed Sep 12 14:29:35 PDT 2007

# xorg.conf (xorg X Window System server configuration file)
#
# This file was generated by dexconf, the Debian X Configuration tool, using
# values from the debconf database.
#
# Edit this file with caution, and see the xorg.conf manual page.
# (Type "man xorg.conf" at the shell prompt.)
#
# This file is automatically updated on xserver-xorg package upgrades *only*
# if it has not been modified since the last upgrade of the xserver-xorg
# package.
#
# If you have edited this file but would like it to be automatically updated
# again, run the following command:
#   sudo dpkg-reconfigure -phigh xserver-xorg

Section "ServerLayout"

	# Uncomment if you have a wacom tablet
	#	InputDevice     "stylus"	"SendCoreEvents"
	#	InputDevice     "cursor"	"SendCoreEvents"
	#	InputDevice     "eraser"	"SendCoreEvents"
    Identifier     "Default Layout"
    Screen         "Default Screen" 0 0
    InputDevice    "Generic Keyboard"
    InputDevice    "Configured Mouse"
    InputDevice    "Synaptics Touchpad"
EndSection

Section "Files"
EndSection

Section "Module"
    Load           "glx"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
    Identifier     "Generic Keyboard"
    Driver         "kbd"
    Option         "CoreKeyboard"
    Option         "XkbRules" "xorg"
    Option         "XkbModel" "pc105"
    Option         "XkbLayout" "de"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
    Identifier     "Configured Mouse"
    Driver         "mouse"
    Option         "CorePointer"
    Option         "Device" "/dev/input/mice"
    Option         "Protocol" "ImPS/2"
    Option         "ZAxisMapping" "4 5"
    Option         "Emulate3Buttons" "true"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
    Identifier     "Synaptics Touchpad"
    Driver         "synaptics"
    Option         "SendCoreEvents" "true"
    Option         "Device" "/dev/psaux"
    Option         "Protocol" "auto-dev"
    Option         "HorizEdgeScroll" "0"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
    Identifier     "stylus"
    Driver         "wacom"
    Option         "Device" "/dev/input/wacom"
    Option         "Type" "stylus"
    Option         "ForceDevice" "ISDV4"# Tablet PC ONLY
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
    Identifier     "eraser"
    Driver         "wacom"
    Option         "Device" "/dev/input/wacom"
    Option         "Type" "eraser"
    Option         "ForceDevice" "ISDV4"# Tablet PC ONLY
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
    Identifier     "cursor"
    Driver         "wacom"
    Option         "Device" "/dev/input/wacom"
    Option         "Type" "cursor"
    Option         "ForceDevice" "ISDV4"# Tablet PC ONLY
EndSection

Section "Monitor"
    Identifier     "Standardbildschirm"
    HorizSync       28.0 - 64.0
    VertRefresh     43.0 - 60.0
    Option         "DPMS"
EndSection

Section "Device"
    Identifier     "nVidia Corporation NV43 [GeForce Go 6200/6400]"
    Driver         "nvidia"
EndSection

Section "Screen"
    Identifier     "Default Screen"
    Device         "nVidia Corporation NV43 [GeForce Go 6200/6400]"
    Monitor        "Standardbildschirm"
    DefaultDepth    24
    Option         "AddARGBVisuals" "True"
    Option         "NoLogo" "True"
    Option         "AddARGBGLXVisuals" "True"
    SubSection     "Display"
        Depth       24
        Modes      "1280x800"
    EndSubSection
EndSection

```

More information in the screenshot.


I need help. :( Does anybody known why this happens?

### Post by Steven_B on 2007-11-10
Does it start out using that much ram?
I've been having a problem where everything starts ok, but as I use the system, it eats up all my ram until the system becomes very slow.  The attached screenshot shows how much ram I got back when I tuned off compiz.
I have an nVidia NVS 110m (in a laptop), using the nvidia-glx-new drivers.

---

### Post by Kimmik on 2007-11-10
> **Steven_B said:**
> Does it start out using that much ram?
I've been having a problem where everything starts ok, but as I use the system, it eats up all my ram until the system becomes very slow.  The attached screenshot shows how much ram I got back when I tuned off compiz.
I have an nVidia NVS 110m (in a laptop), using the nvidia-glx-new drivers.

Yes, I experienced the same.
Solution: Start compiz with indirect rendering!

```
/usr/bin/compiz.real --ignore-desktop-hints --replace --indirect-rendering --sm-client-id default0 ccp
```

---

### Post by chrisccoulson on 2007-11-10
Thats not really a solution, but more of a workaround though. Do you have any other plugins enabled in Compiz other than the default? Could you list which plugins you are using?

I had a problem with excessive RAM usage, and I managed to narrow it down to the Skydome. It would probably be useful if you could do something similar

---

### Post by RFScheer on 2007-11-10
The indirect rendering option works for me.  No more creeping increase of RAM use on repeated window opening/closing.  Thanks for the tip.

I do get a warning message from compiz.real 

> Warn: No 8 bit GLX pixmap format, disabling YV12 image format


But it works anyway.

---

I tried the indirect rendering and I got the same error:

Compiz works but is so slow it is not worth using.

I have a Geforce 6150 integrated video card.
